properties.remark: A family of thin waterspouts were observed by an NWS employee to the southwest of Fort Zachary Taylor State Park in Key West. Four waterspouts were observed, with the longest lasting around 8 minutes. No spray ring was visible from the waterspouts.
A WNW wind gust of 42 knots, or 48 mph, was measured by the Sand Key C-Man station at 940 am EST along a thundery squall.
A wind gust of 37 knots, or 43 mph, was measured by the Sombrero Key C-MAN station at 1020 am EST along a thundery squall.
A wind gust of 45 knots, or 52 mph, was measured at the Carysfort Reef Light WeatherFlow mesonet at 1120 am EST along a thundery squall.
The Sombrero Key C-MAN measured a 47-knot wind gust.
The Long Key C-MAN measured a 37 knot wind gust.
The Weatherflow mesonet sensor at Carysfort Reef measured a 47 knot wind gust.
A west northwest wind gusts of 41 knots, or 47 mph, was measured at the Sand Key C-MAN at 210 PM EST.
A waterspout over Cudjoe Bay moved onshore at around 12:02 pm EST in Cudjoe Key. At least one structure sustained damage on Malibu Round-A-Bout.
A west wind gust of 36 knots, or 41 mph, was measured at the Sand Key C-Man Station at 12:30 PM EST.

Corrects time forward an hour for previous tornado report from Cudjoe Key. A waterspout over Cudjoe Bay moved onshore at around 1:02 pm EST in Cudjoe Key. At least one structure sustained damage on Malibu Round-A-Bout.
A west wind gust of 36 knots, or 40 mph, was measured by the CWOP mesonet station at Cudjoe Key at 109 PM EST.
A southwesterly wind gust of 44 knots, or 51 mph, was measured by the Sombrero Key C-MAN station at 1:40 PM EST.
A south southwest wind gust of 37 knots, or 43 mph, was measured by the Long Key C-Man station at 2:10 PM EST.
The Weatherflow mesonet sensor at Careysfort Reef measured a 40 knot, or 46 mph, wind gust.
A southwest wind gust of 35 knots, or 40 mph, was measured by the Sand Key C-MAN Station at 9:10 pm.
A northwest wind gust of 44 knots, or 51 mph, was measured by the Sand Key C-Man station at 9:20 pm.
A west southwest wind gust of 53 knots, or 61 mph, was measured by the Sombrero Key C-MAN station at 10:20 pm EST.

Two waterspouts were observed by an off-duty Florida Keys NWS meteorologist south of the Channel 5 Bridge around 4:00 PM EDT. One had a visible spray ring. Movement was estimated to be east at 10 to 15 knots; duration unknown.

Social media reports of an early morning tornado on Little Torch were investigated by NWS personnel. The brief Storm Survey revealed two downed trees, broken small and medium-sized live branches, a destroyed plastic storage shed, and three destroyed/twisted breakaway shade canopies. There was no damage to structures. Path length estimated at .25 miles to the east-southeast of the initial damage. Time estimated from radar, which corresponds well with an eyewitness report.
The Weatherflow mesonet sensor at Careysfort Reef measured a 35 knots, or 40 mph, wind gust associated with a thunderstorm.
A southwest wind gust of 50 knots, or 58 mph, was measured by the Long Key C-MAN Station at 8:00 AM EDT.
A southwest wind gust of 52 knots, or 60 mph, was measured by the Alligator Reef Light Weatherflow mesonet station at 8:14 AM EDT.
A southwest wind gust of 36 knots, or 41 mph, was measured by the Carysfort Reef Light Weatherflow mesonet at 9:10 AM EDT.
The Weatherflow mesonet at Alligator Reef measured a 35 knot wind gust during a recent squall.
The Long Key C-MAN station measured a 35 knot wind gust during a recent squall.
A wind gust of 35 knots, or 40 mph, was measured by the Long Key CMAN station at 900 AM.
A wind gust of 34 knots, or 39 mph, was measured by the Long Key CMAN station at 910 AM.
A southerly wind gust of 36 knots, or 41 mph, was measured by the Sand Key CMAN station at 9:20 am.
Corrects previous marine tstm wind report from Sand Key Light. A wind gust of 39 knots, or 45 mph, was measured by the Sand Key Light C-MAN Station at 9:50 AM.
Mesonet station SANF1 9 SW Key West.
A southwesterly wind gust of 35 knots, or 40 mph, was measured by the Sombrero Key C-MAN Station at 10:00 AM.
A wind gust of 40 knots, or 46 mph, was measured by the Sand Key C-MAN station at 10:00 AM.
A southwesterly wind gust of 38 knots, or 44 mph, was measured by the Sombrero Key C-MAN station at 10:10 AM.
A southwesterly wind gust of 34 knots, or 39 mph, was measured by the Long Key C-MAN Station at 10:20 AM.
A southwesterly wind gust of 36 knots, or 42 mph, was measured by the Carysfort Reef Light Weatherflow mesonet station at 10:35 AM.

A waterspout was observed by an off-duty Florida Keys NWS meteorologist south of the Boca Chica bridge at 1:12 PM EDT. No spray ring was observed and the funnel was halfway down. Movement and duration unknown.
A member of the public reported several weak waterspouts south of Boca Chica. Each waterspout was thin and very short-lived, with no spray ring detected.

NWS Employee reports seeing two waterspouts southwest of Bahia Honda Key. One waterspout extended about a quarter to a third of the way down to the surface. Very narrow in shape with no spray ring observed. Second waterspout was also narrow, only extending about a fifth of the way down to the surface with no spray ring observed. At the time of the report, the waterspouts had a duration of 10 mins and were still ongoing.
Sand Key reports measured wind gust of 34 knots with marine thunderstorm at 540 am.
The Weatherflow mesonet sensor at Alligator Reef measured at 37 knot wind gust at 612 AM.

At 715 AM, a waterspout was observed just northwest of Key West via boaters and confirmed by NWS employees. The waterspout lasted approximately 30 minutes and was described as thick. Could not confirm a spray ring due to the base of the waterspout being obscured.

Corrects previous waterspout report from 2 WNW Key West. At 715 AM, a waterspout was observed just northwest of Key West via boaters and confirmed by NWS employees. The waterspout lasted approximately 30 minutes and was described as thick. Edit: Photographs confirmed the presence of a spray ring.
The CWOP on Sugarloaf Key measured a 38 knot wind gust from the southwest during a thunderstorm collapse.

At 655 PM a small family of waterspouts was observed just northwest of Howe Key by local residents. Photos and spoken report confirm a straight, thick, and dark funnel with a spray ring for the largest of the bunch with a couple smaller rope like spouts in the background. Once observed, waterspouts persisted for about 10 minutes before dissipating.

At approximately 3:20 PM EDT, the Monroe Count Sheriffs office called in a report of a waterspout to the west of the Channel 5 Bridge. The waterspout was reported to the Sheriffs office by a member of the public, and the sheriffs office reported it to us on their behalf. No other information was available.

Received a photo of a waterspout from a member of the public via Facebook at 11:38 AM EDT. The person who took the photo was in Cudjoe Bay, facing south. Radar at that time showed a cell to the south of Cudjoe Bay with a core of 40dbz to 60dbz.

A member of the public reported a waterspout with a photo via Twitter/X. The photo was taken at approximately 1:00 PM EDT, and the reporter said they were on Key West and looking roughly southeastward. The horizon is obstructed with trees, but the photo has a thin condensation funnel that is reaching approximately halfway down.
A southerly wind gust of 40 knots, or 46 mph, was measured by the Carysfort Reef Light Weatherflow mesonet station at 0219 AM.
A southerly wind gust of 40 knots, or 46 mph, was measured by the Sand Key C-MAN Station at 1:20 PM.
A SW wind gust of 41 knots, or 47 mph, was measured by the Sand Key C-Man Station at 730 pm EDT.
A southerly wind gust of 39 knots, or 44 mph, was recorded by the Carysfort Reef Light Weatherflow Mesonet Station at 0932 AM.
A southerly wind gust of 34 knots, or 39 mph, was recorded at Carysfort Reef Light Weatherflow Mesonet station at 0949 AM.
A northwest wind gust of 40 knots, or 46 mph, was measured by the Carysfort Reef Light Weatherflow mesonet at 7:19 PM EDT.
A NE wind gusts of 34 knots, or 39 mph, was measured by the Long Key C-MAN station at 9:00 pm EDT.
A SW wind gust of 37 knots, or 43 mph, was measured by the Sand Key C-Man Station at 600 pm EDT.
A SW wind gust of 34 knots, or 39 mph, was measured by the Key West International Airport ASOS station at 616 pm EDT.
The Boca Chica ASOS measured a 34 knot wind gust during a recent squall.

At 3:18 PM EDT, a member of the public submitted a photo of a waterspout occurring just off of the coast of Marathon on the bay side. A trained spotter also called in the same waterspout, but they also informed us that they could see the spray ring at the surface as they were out on the water fishing.
A northeasterly wind gust of 34 knots, or 39 mph, was measured by the Sand Key C-Man Station at 1000 EDT.
A northeast wind gust of 40 knots, or 46 mph, was measured by the Carysfort Reef Light Weatherflow station at 1129 PM EDT.
NW wind gust of 34 knots was recorded at the Alligator Reef Light WeatherFlow mesonet site.
A NW wind gust of 40 knots was recorded by the Carysfort Reef Light WeatherFlow mesonet site.

A skinny, tilted waterspout was observed from the rooftop of the Florida Keys NWS from 6:50-6:55 PM EDT. The condensation cloud extended about one fifth of the way from the cloud base to the horizon. View of the surface was obstructed so it is unknown if a spray ring was present. Movement was to the west-northwest around 10 knots. Duration was approximately 5 minutes before it dissipated. Location estimated based on radar.

Corrects previous Tstm Wnd Dmg report from 4 NE Windley Key. A wet microburst impacted portions of the Indian Mound community of Plantation Key around 11 PM EDT on Sunday, July 21st. Numerous trees and limbs were downed across the area as the microburst delivered winds estimated to be between 60-85 mph based on damage photos and videos. A personal weather station south of this area recorded a wind gust of 61 mph as the thunderstorm approached the island chain. PREVIOUS INFORMATION: Approximately 1100 residents were without power. Monroe County Sheriff Office reported a tree down on US 1.

Numerous trees reported down on Plantation Key. Approximately 1100 residents were without power. Monroe County Sherriff Office reported a tree down on US 1. Time approximated at 11 PM based on radar data.
A NE wind gust of 34 knots, or 39 mph, was recorded by the Long Key C-MAN station at 910 AM EDT.
A E wind gust of 38 knots, or 44 mph, was recorded by the Carysfort Reef Light Weatherflow mesonet site.
An east northeasterly wind gust of 36 knots, or 42 mph, was recorded by the Alligator Reef Light WeatherFlow mesonet site.
An east northeasterly wind gust of 39 knots, or 45 mph, was recorded by the Alligator Reef Light WeatherFlow mesonet site at 915 PM EDT.
Reports of a tree down blocking the road.
A southwest wind gust of 52 knots, or 60 mph, was measured by the Alligator Reef Light mesonet station at 1645 EDT.
A south to southwest wind gusts of 48 knots, or 55 mph, was measured by the C-MAN station at Long Key at 1650 EDT.
A southwesterly wind gust of 40 knots, or 46 mph, was measured by the Carysfort Reef Light WeatherFlow station at 1754 EDT.
A westerly wind gust of 38 knots, or 44 mph, was measured by the Carysfort Reef Light Weatherflow station at 641 AM EDT.

A waterspout was observed bayside of Marathon, moving eastward around 10 knots. The thin, tilted condensation cloud extended 3/4 of the way from the cloud base to the horizon, but view of the surface was obstructed so it was unknown if a spray ring was present. Location estimated based on satellite imagery. Duration was approximately 10 minutes.

Corrects previous waterspout report from 2 NNW Key Colony Beach, upon receipt of additional photos that show a spray ring. A mature waterspout was observed bayside of Marathon, moving eastward around 10 knots. The thin, tilted condensation cloud extended all of the way from the cloud base to the surface with a robust spray ring present. Location estimated based on satellite imagery. Duration was approximately 10 minutes.

Two waterspouts were observed on the Atlantic side of Key Colony Beach and Coco Plum Beach, moving eastward around 10 knots. The generally thin, tilted condensation clouds extended 1/4 of the way from the cloud base to the horizon, but view of the surface was obstructed so it was unknown if spray rings were present. Location estimated based on satellite imagery. Duration was approximately 5 minutes for each one.
A southwest wind gust of 47 knots, or 54 mph, was measured by the Sand Key Light C-MAN stations at 0700 P EDT.
An NWS employee observed a waterspout in the Gulf waters near Sugarloaf Key. The waterspout was moderately sized and tilted. It lasted a few minutes before dissipating.

A skinny, tilted waterspout was observed by the range tower near the Northwest Channel. The condensation cloud extended about a third of the way from the cloud base to the surface and no spray ring was observed. Movement was slowly WSW and the duration was approximately 7 minutes.

A skinny, tilted waterspout was observed by a member of the public just east of No Name Key. The condensation cloud almost extends completely to the surface, but no spray ring was observed. The waterspout lasted a little over a minute before dissipating.
An ENE wind gust of 38 knots, or 44 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet site at Alligator Reef Light at 120 PM EDT.
An ESE wind gust of 38 knots, or 44 mph, was recorded by the Long Key C-MAN station at 150 PM EDT.

Delayed report of a waterspout via multiple social media accounts. The latitude and longitude based on subjective descriptions of location combined with radar data. The waterspout had a well developed collar cloud and a stout cone-shaped funnel. Spray ring undetermined.
A NNE wind gust of 37 knots, or 43 mph, was recorded by the Sand Key C-MAN station at 3:00 PM EDT.
A southeasterly wind gust of 34 knots, or 39 mph, was measured by the Carysfort Reef Light Weatherflow station at 1125 AM EDT.

A mature waterspout was observed south of Key West around 12:35 PM EDT. The condensation cloud extended almost completely to the surface. A spray ring was visible. Movement was slowly to the north. Duration was at least 10 minutes before it became obscured by rain and was no longer visible. Location estimated via radar.

Corrects previous waterspout report from 2 N Bahia Honda Key to update time of report, include a spray ring and add a duration. An off-duty Florida Keys NWS employee observed a waterspout NE of Bahia Honda State Park at 3:55 PM EDT. It was a thin, rope-like waterspout that extended all of the way from the cloud base to the surface. Video from another viewer shows a robust spray ring. Duration was approximately 10 minutes.

An off-duty Florida Keys NWS employee observed a waterspout NE of Bahia Honda State Park at 3:55 PM EDT. It was a thin, rope-like waterspout that extended all of the way from the cloud base to the horizon. View of the surface was obscured by mangroves so it was unknown if a spray ring was present. Duration unknown.

Three thin, rope-like waterspouts were observed south of Summerland Key between 5:00 and 5:20 PM EDT. One waterspout had a condensation cloud that extended all of the way from the cloud base to the surface, with a spray ring visible. One waterspout had a condensation cloud that extended about half-way from the cloud base to the surface, also with a spray ring visible. The last waterspout had a white condensation cloud that extended about a third of the way from the cloud base to the surface, with no spray ring visible. Movement was slowly to the east-southeast. Each waterspout had a duration of approximately 5 to 6 minutes.
A wind gust of 35 knots, or 40 mph, was measured by the Carysfort Reef Light Weatherflow mesonet station at 904 PM EST.
A wind gust of 45 knots, or 52 mph, was measured by the Carysfort Reef Light Weatherflow mesonet station at 344 pm EDT.
An east wind gust of 39 knots, or 44 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet site at Carysfort Reef Light at 6:09 PM EDT.

An ESE wind gust of 36 knots, or 41 mph, was recorded by the Key West International Airport ASOS at 6:23 PM EDT. This gust was associated with the first outer rainband from Tropical Storm Helene, located south of the western tip of Cuba.

A south wind gust of 40 knots, or 46 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet site XCFL at Carysfort Reef Light. This gust was associated with an outer rainband from Hurricane Helene, off to the distant WSW.

A nearly-vertical, elephant trunk-like, mature waterspout was reported via social media, south of Toms Harbor Cut Bridge. The condensation cloud extended all of the way from the cloud base to the surface and a spray ring was present. Movement was northwestward around 10 to 15 knots. Duration is unknown at this time.

Updates previous waterspout report from 3 SSE Duck Key. A nearly-vertical, elephant trunk-like, mature waterspout was reported via social media, south of Toms Harbor Cut Bridge. The condensation cloud extended all of the way from the cloud base to the surface and a spray ring was present. Movement was northwestward around 10 to 15 knots. Duration is now estimated to be around 15 minutes.
A WSW wind gust of 34 knots, or 39 mph, was recorded by the C-MAN station at Sand Key Light SANF1.
A SW wind gust of 37 knots, or 43 mph, was recorded by the Keys Energy Big Coppitt Electric Substation mesonet site.
A SE wind gust of 35 knots, or 40 mph, was recorded by the Weatherflow mesonet station in Cudjoe Key.
A W wind gust of 36 knots, or 41 mph, was recorded the the Long Key C-MAN station.
A W wind gust of 38 knots, or 44 mph, was recorded by the C-MAN station at Sand Key Light SANF1.
A W wind gust of 34 knots, or 39 mph, was recorded at the Weatherflow Mesonet site at Carysfort Reef Light.

A long-duration waterspout was observed from the roof of the Florida Keys NWS office from 6:51-7:18 PM EDT. It appeared tilted throughout its lifespan, was generally skinny, and the condensation cloud extended about a fifth of the way from the cloud base to the horizon at the furthest extent. View of the surface was obscured so it is unknown if a spray ring was present. Movement was west-southwest around 10 knots. Duration was 27 minutes.

A waterspout was observed from a webcam on Pigeon Key at 12:55 pm. The condensation cloud extended about halfway from the cloud base to the horizon at the furthest known extent. View of the surface was obscured so it is unknown if a spray ring was present. Duration is unknown.
An ESE wind gust of 36 knots, or 41 mph, was measured by the WeatherFlow mesonet site XALG at Alligator Reef Light.
A SE wind gust of 37 knots, or 43 mph, was measured by the Long Key C-MAN station.
An easterly wind gust of 34 knots, or 39 mph, from a thunderstorm was measured by the XALG Alligator Reef Light WeatherFlow mesonet station.
An easterly wind gust of 34 knots, or 39 mph, from a thunderstorm was measured by the XALG Alligator Reef Light Weatherflow mesonet station.
An ENE wind gust of 34 knots, or 39 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet site XALG at Alligator Reef Light.
